description: The physical and social unit of those living together in a shared space. It is the immediate sphere of domestic life and mutual support.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'ho' (a warm, receptive greeting) and 'ma' (grounded presence). The word's structure suggests 'a grounded, receptive space.' This frames the household as a place of welcome and stability. It is phonetically related to 'homi' (welcome), reinforcing the idea of a household as the primary place of welcome.
